daily_robot.title: Unified 4D World Action Modeling from Video Priors with Asynchronous Denoising
daily_robot.abstract: We propose X-WAM, a Unified 4D World Model that unifies real-time robotic action execution and high-fidelity 4D world synthesis (video + 3D reconstruction) in a single framework, addressing the critical limitations of prior unified world models (e.g., UWM) that only model 2D pixel-space and fail to balance action efficiency and world modeling quality. To leverage the strong visual priors of pretrained video diffusion models, X-WAM imagines the future world by predicting multi-view RGB-D videos, and obtains spatial information efficiently through a lightweight structural adaptation: replicating the final few blocks of the pretrained Diffusion Transformer into a dedicated depth prediction branch for the reconstruction of future spatial information. Moreover, we propose Asynchronous Noise Sampling (ANS) to jointly optimize generation quality and action decoding efficiency. ANS applies a specialized asynchronous denoising schedule during inference, which rapidly decodes actions with fewer steps to enable efficient real-time execution, while dedicating the full sequence of steps to generate high-fidelity video. Rather than entirely decoupling the timesteps during training, ANS samples from their joint distribution to align with the inference distribution. Pretrained on over 5,800 hours of robotic data, X-WAM achieves 79.2% and 90.7% average success rate on RoboCasa and RoboTwin 2.0 benchmarks, while producing high-fidelity 4D reconstruction and generation surpassing existing methods in both visual and geometric metrics.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 现有统一世界模型（如UWM）仅建模2D像素空间，难以平衡动作执行效率与世界建模质量。本文提出X-WAM，利用预训练视频扩散模型的视觉先验，通过轻量结构适配（复制DiT最后几块为深度预测分支）预测多视图RGB-D视频，并设计异步噪声采样（ANS）联合优化生成质量与动作解码效率。在5800小时机器人数据上预训练，X-WAM在RoboCasa和RoboTwin 2.0基准上分别达到79.2%和90.7%平均成功率，4D重建与生成在视觉和几何指标上均超越现有方法。

daily_robot.title: Three-Step Nav: A Hierarchical Global-Local Planner for Zero-Shot Vision-and-Language Navigation
daily_robot.abstract: Breakthrough progress in vision-based navigation through unknown environments has been achieved by using multimodal large language models (MLLMs). These models can plan a sequence of motions by evaluating the current view at each time step against the task and goal given to the agent. However, current zero-shot Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) agents powered by MLLMs still tend to drift off course, halt prematurely, and achieve low overall success rates. We propose Three-Step Nav to counteract these failures with a three-view protocol: First, "look forward" to extract global landmarks and sketch a coarse plan. Then, "look now" to align the current visual observation with the next sub-goal for fine-grained guidance. Finally, "look backward" audits the entire trajectory to correct accumulated drift before stopping. Requiring no gradient updates or task-specific fine-tuning, our planner drops into existing VLN pipelines with minimal overhead. Three-Step Nav achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on the R2R-CE and RxR-CE dataset. Our code is available at https://github.com/ZoeyZheng0/3-step-Nav.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 当前零样本视觉-语言导航（VLN）代理存在易漂移、过早停止和成功率低的问题。本文提出Three-Step Nav，采用“前视—当前视—后视”三级分层规划：前视提取全局地标并生成粗略路径，当前视将当前观察与子目标对齐提供细粒度引导，后视审计整个轨迹以纠正累积漂移。该方法无需梯度更新或任务微调，可轻量嵌入现有VLN管道。在R2R-CE和RxR-CE数据集上取得了零样本SOTA性能。

daily_robot.title: Bi-Level Optimization for Contact and Motion Planning in Rope-Assisted Legged Robots
daily_robot.abstract: This paper presents a planning pipeline framework for locomotion in rope-assisted robots climbing vertical surfaces. The proposed framework is formulated as a bi-level optimization scheme that addresses a mixed-integer problem: selecting feasible terrain regions for landing while simultaneously optimizing the control inputs, namely rope tensions and leg forces, and landing location. The outer level of the optimization is solved using the Cross-Entropy Method, while the inner level relies on gradient-based nonlinear optimization to compute dynamically feasible motions. The approach is validated on a novel climbing robot platform, ALPINE, across a variety of challenging terrain configurations.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 这篇论文针对绳索辅助攀爬机器人在垂直表面移动时的接触与运动规划问题，提出一种双层优化规划框架。该方法将混合整数问题分解为外层采用交叉熵方法选择可行着陆区域，内层基于梯度的非线性优化计算动态可行运动，实现绳索张力、腿部力和着陆位置的协同优化。在新型攀爬机器人平台ALPINE上的多种复杂地形验证了该框架的有效性。

daily_robot.title: Safe Navigation using Neural Radiance Fields via Reachable Sets
daily_robot.abstract: Safe navigation in cluttered environments is an important challenge for autonomous systems. Robots navigating through obstacle ridden scenarios need to be able to navigate safely in the presence of obstacles, goals, and ego objects of varying geometries. In this work, reachable set representations of the robot's real-time capabilities in the state space can be utilized to capture safe navigation requirements. While neural radiance fields (NeRFs) are utilized to compute, store, and manipulate the volumetric representations of the obstacles, or ego vehicle, as needed. Constrained optimal control is employed to represent the resulting path planning problem, involving linear matrix inequality constraints. We present simulation results for path planning in the presence of numerous obstacles in two different scenarios. Safe navigation is demonstrated through using reachable sets in the corresponding constrained optimal control problems.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 这篇论文旨在解决杂乱环境中自主系统的安全导航问题。方法上，利用可达集表示机器人实时能力，并结合神经辐射场（NeRF）计算和存储障碍物的体积表示，通过含线性矩阵不等式约束的最优控制进行路径规划。创新点在于将NeRF与可达集结合用于安全导航约束。仿真实验在两种不同场景下验证了该方法能有效实现安全导航。

daily_robot.title: Stochastic Entanglement of Deterministic Origami Tentacles For Universal Robotic Gripping
daily_robot.abstract: Origami-inspired robotic grippers have shown promising potential for object manipulation tasks due to their compact volume and mechanical flexibility. However, robust capture of objects with random shapes in dynamic working environments often comes at the cost of additional actuation channels and control complexity. Here, we introduce a tendon-driven origami tentacle gripper capable of universal object gripping by exploiting a synergy between local, deterministic deformation programming and global, stochastic entanglements. Each origami tentacle is made by cutting thin Mylar sheets; It features carefully placed holes for routing an actuation tendon, origami creases for controlling the deformation, and a tapered shape. By tailoring these design features, one can prescribe the shrinking, bending, and twisting deformation, eventually creating deterministic coiling with a simple tendon pull. Then, when multiple coiling tentacles are placed in proximity, stochastic entanglement emerges, allowing the tentacles to braid, knot, and grip objects with random shapes. We derived a simulation model by integrating origami mechanics with Cosserat rods to correlate origami design, tendon deformation, and their collective gripping performance. Then, we experimentally tested how these coiling and entangling origami tentacles can grasp objects under gravity and in water. A stow-and-release deployment mechanism was also tested to simulate in-orbit grasping. Overall, the entertaining origami tentacle gripper presents a new strategy for robust object grasping with simple design and actuation.
daily_robot.summary_zh:
核心问题：解决动态环境中随机形状物体的鲁棒抓取问题，避免额外驱动通道和控制复杂性。  
方法与创新点：提出肌腱驱动折纸触手，通过局部确定性变形（卷曲）与全局随机缠绕（编织、打结）协同实现通用抓取，并融合折纸力学与Cosserat杆建立仿真模型。  
关键结论：实验验证了该抓手能在重力、水下及模拟在轨环境中可靠抓取随机形状物体，以简单设计和驱动实现鲁棒抓取。

daily_robot.title: Edge AI for Automotive Vulnerable Road User Safety: Deployable Detection via Knowledge Distillation
daily_robot.abstract: Deploying accurate object detection for Vulnerable Road User (VRU) safety on edge hardware requires balancing model capacity against computational constraints. Large models achieve high accuracy but fail under INT8 quantization required for edge deployment, while small models sacrifice detection performance. This paper presents a knowledge distillation (KD) framework that trains a compact YOLOv8-S student (11.2M parameters) to mimic a YOLOv8-L teacher (43.7M parameters), achieving 3.9x compression while preserving quantization robustness. We evaluate on full-scale BDD100K (70K training images) with Post-Training Quantization to INT8. The teacher suffers catastrophic degradation under INT8 (-23% mAP), while the KD student retains accuracy (-5.6% mAP). Analysis reveals that KD transfers precision calibration rather than raw detection capacity: the KD student achieves 0.748 precision versus 0.653 for direct training at INT8, a 14.5% gain at equivalent recall, reducing false alarms by 44% versus the collapsed teacher. At INT8, the KD student exceeds the teacher's FP32 precision (0.748 vs. 0.718) in a model 3.9x smaller. These findings establish knowledge distillation as a requirement for deploying accurate, safety-critical VRU detection on edge hardware.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 针对边缘硬件部署弱势道路使用者（VRU）安全检测，大模型在INT8量化下性能严重退化（mAP下降23%），小模型精度不足。本文提出知识蒸馏（KD）框架，训练紧凑YOLOv8-S学生（11.2M参数）模仿YOLOv8-L教师（43.7M参数），实现3.9倍压缩并保持量化鲁棒性。在BDD100K数据集上，KD学生INT8下mAP仅降5.6%，精度0.748，较直接训练提升14.5%，虚警减少44%，且学生INT8精度（0.748）超越教师FP32精度（0.718）。

daily_robot.title: STARRY: Spatial-Temporal Action-Centric World Modeling for Robotic Manipulation
daily_robot.abstract: Robotic manipulation critically requires reasoning about future spatial-temporal interactions, yet existing VLA policies and world-model-enhanced policies do not fully model action-relevant spatial-temporal interaction structure. We propose STARRY, a world-model-enhanced action-generation policy that aligns spatial-temporal prediction with action generation. STARRY jointly denoises future spatial-temporal latents and action sequences, and introduces Geometry-Aware Selective Attention Modulation to convert predicted depth and end-effector geometry into token-aligned weights for selective action-attention modulation. On RoboTwin 2.0, STARRY achieves 93.82% / 93.30% average success under Clean and Randomized settings. Real-world experiments further improve average success from 42.5% to 70.8% over $π_{0.5}$, demonstrating the effectiveness of action-centric spatial-temporal world modeling for spatial-temporally demanding robotic action generation.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 核心问题是现有方法未充分建模机器人操作中动作相关的时空交互结构。本文提出 STARRY，一种世界模型增强的动作生成策略，通过联合去噪未来时空潜变量与动作序列，并引入几何感知选择性注意力调制，将预测深度和末端执行器几何信息转化为令牌对齐权重进行选择性动作注意力调制。在 RoboTwin 2.0 上，清洁与随机设置下平均成功率分别达 93.82% 和 93.30%；真实实验中，相较 π₀.₅ 将平均成功率从 42.5% 提升至 70.8%，验证了动作中心时空世界模型在需求高时空精度的机器人动作生成中的有效性。

daily_robot.title: Walk With Me: Long-Horizon Social Navigation for Human-Centric Outdoor Assistance
daily_robot.abstract: Assisting humans in open-world outdoor environments requires robots to translate high-level natural-language intentions into safe, long-horizon, and socially compliant navigation behavior. Existing map-based methods rely on costly pre-built HD maps, while learning-based policies are mostly limited to indoor and short-horizon settings. To bridge this gap, we propose Walk with Me, a map-free framework for long-horizon social navigation from high-level human instructions. Walk with Me leverages GPS context and lightweight candidate points-of-interest from a public map API for semantic destination grounding and waypoint proposal. A High-Level Vision-Language Model grounds abstract instructions into concrete destinations and plans coarse waypoint sequences. During execution, an observation-aware routing mechanism determines whether the Low-Level Vision-Language-Action policy can handle the current situation or whether explicit safety reasoning from the High-Level VLM is needed. Routine segments are executed by the Low-Level VLA, while complex situations such as crowded crossings trigger high-level reasoning and stop-and-wait behavior when unsafe. By combining semantic intent grounding, map-free long-horizon planning, safety-aware reasoning, and low-level action generation, Walk with Me enables practical outdoor social navigation for human-centric assistance.
daily_robot.summary_zh:
核心问题：机器人如何在无高精地图的户外开放环境中，根据高层自然语言指令实现长时程、安全且符合社交规范的导航？

方法与创新点：提出Walk with Me框架，利用GPS和公共地图API的轻量候选兴趣点进行语义目的地定位与航点提议；高层视觉语言模型（VLM）将抽象指令转化为具体目的地并规划粗粒度航点，低层视觉-语言-动作策略（VLA）执行常规路径，同时通过观测感知路由机制在复杂情形（如拥挤路口）触发高层安全推理和停止等待行为。

关键结论：结合语义意图定位、无地图长时程规划、安全推理与低层动作生成

daily_robot.title: Rule-based High-Level Coaching for Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning in Search-and-Rescue UAV Missions Under Limited-Simulation Training
daily_robot.abstract: This paper presents a hierarchical decision-making framework for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) missions motivated by search-and-rescue (SAR) scenarios under limited simulation training. The framework combines a fixed rule-based high-level advisor with an online goal-conditioned low-level reinforcement learning (RL) controller. To stress-test early adaptation, we also consider a strict no-pretraining deployment regime. The high-level advisor is defined offline from a structured task specification and compiled into deterministic rules. It provides interpretable mission- and safety-aware guidance through recommended actions, avoided actions, and regime-dependent arbitration weights. The low-level controller learns online from task-defined dense rewards and reuses experience through a mode-aware prioritized replay mechanism augmented with rule-derived metadata. We evaluate the framework on two tasks: battery-aware multi-goal delivery and moving-target delivery in obstacle-rich environments. Across both tasks, the proposed method improves early safety and sample efficiency primarily by reducing collision terminations, while preserving the ability to adapt online to scenario-specific dynamics.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 这篇论文针对有限仿真训练下搜救无人机任务，提出一种结合固定规则高层顾问与在线目标条件低层强化学习控制器的分层决策框架。创新点在于规则顾问提供可解释的安全引导与模式感知优先级重放机制。实验证明该方法能减少碰撞终止，提升早期安全性和样本效率。

daily_robot.title: Atomic-Probe Governance for Skill Updates in Compositional Robot Policies
daily_robot.abstract: Skill libraries in deployed robotic systems are continually updated through fine-tuning, fresh demonstrations, or domain adaptation, yet existing typed-composition methods (BLADE, SymSkill, Generative Skill Chaining) treat the library as frozen at test time and do not analyze how composition outcomes change when a skill is replaced. We introduce a paired-sampling cross-version swap protocol on robosuite manipulation tasks to characterize this dimension of compositional skill learning. On a dual-arm peg-in-hole task we discover a dominant-skill effect: one ECM achieves 86.7% atomic success rate while every other ECM is at or below 26.7%, and whether this dominant ECM enters a composition shifts the success rate by up to +50pp. We characterize the boundary on a simpler pick task where all atomic policies saturate at 100% and the effect is undefined. Across three tasks we further find that off-policy behavioral distance metrics fail to identify the dominant ECM, ruling out the natural cheap predictor. We propose an atomic-quality probe and a Hybrid Selector combining per-skill probes (zero per-decision cost) with selective composition revalidation (full cost), and characterize its Pareto frontier on 144 skill-update decisions. On T6 the atomic-only probe sits 23pp below full revalidation (64.6% vs 87.5% oracle match) at zero per-decision cost; a Hybrid Selector with m=10 closes most of that gap to ~12pp at 46% of full-revalidation cost. On the cross-task average over 144 events, atomic-only is within 3pp of full revalidation under a mixed-oracle caveat. The atomic-quality probe is, to our knowledge, the first principled, deployment-ready primitive for skill-update governance in compositional robot policies.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 现有组合式技能库在部署后更新时，现有方法视库为冻结，未分析替换技能对组合结果的影响。本文引入配对采样跨版本交换协议，在robosuite任务中发现主导技能效应：双臂销孔任务中一个ECM原子成功率86.7%，其他≤26.7%，其加入使组合成功率提升+50pp。提出原子质量探测器和混合选择器（零成本每技能探针+选择性组合再验证），在144次决策中，

daily_robot.title: ATLAS: An Annotation Tool for Long-horizon Robotic Action Segmentation
daily_robot.abstract: Annotating long-horizon robotic demonstrations with precise temporal action boundaries is crucial for training and evaluating action segmentation and manipulation policy learning methods. Existing annotation tools, however, are often limited: they are designed primarily for vision-only data, do not natively support synchronized visualization of robot-specific time-series signals (e.g., gripper state or force/torque), or require substantial effort to adapt to different dataset formats. In this paper, we introduce ATLAS, an annotation tool tailored for long-horizon robotic action segmentation. ATLAS provides time-synchronized visualization of multi-modal robotic data, including multi-view video and proprioceptive signals, and supports annotation of action boundaries, action labels, and task outcomes. The tool natively handles widely used robotics dataset formats such as ROS bags and the Reinforcement Learning Dataset (RLDS) format, and provides direct support for specific datasets such as REASSEMBLE. ATLAS can be easily extended to new formats via a modular dataset abstraction layer. Its keyboard-centric interface minimizes annotation effort and improves efficiency. In experiments on a contact-rich assembly task, ATLAS reduced the average per-action annotation time by at least 6% compared to ELAN, while the inclusion of time-series data improved temporal alignment with expert annotations by more than 2.8% and decreased boundary error fivefold compared to vision-only annotation tools.
daily_robot.summary_zh:
1. 核心问题：现有标注工具缺乏对机器人多模态时序数据（如力/力矩信号）的原生支持，且难以适配不同数据集格式。  
2. 方法与创新点：提出ATLAS工具，实现多视角视频与本体感觉信号的时间同步可视化，原生支持ROS bag和RLDS等格式，并通过模块化抽象层轻松扩展新格式，采用键盘中心化界面提升效率。  
3. 关键结论：在接触密集装配任务中，ATLAS比ELAN的平均每动作标注时间减少至少6%；相比纯视觉工具，结合时序数据使标注时间对齐精度提升超2.8%，边界误差降低五倍。

daily_robot.title: STAR-Filter: Efficient Convex Free-Space Approximation via Starshaped Set Filtering in Noisy Environments
daily_robot.abstract: Approximating collision-free space is fundamental to robot planning in complex environments. Convex geometric representations, such as polytopes and ellipsoids, are widely employed due to their structural properties, which can be easily integrated with convex optimization. Iterative optimization-based inflation methods can generate large volume polytopes in cluttered environments, but their efficiency degrades as the obstacle set becomes more complex or when sensor data are noisy. These methods are also sensitive to initialization and often rely on accurate geometric models. In this paper, we propose the STAR-Filter, a lightweight framework that employs starshaped set construction as a fast filter for convex region generation in collision-free space. By identifying obstacle points as active supporting constraints, the proposed method significantly reduces redundant computation while preserving feasibility and robustness to sensor noise. We provide theoretical and numerical analyses that characterize the structural properties of the starshaped set and proposed pipeline in environments of varying complexity. Simulation results show that the proposed framework achieves the lowest computation time and reduces conservativeness in polytope generation for real-world noisy and large-scale data. We demonstrate the effectiveness of the framework for Safe Flight Corridor (SFC) generation and agile quadrotor planning in noisy environments.
daily_robot.summary_zh:
核心问题：针对复杂噪声环境中凸自由空间近似效率低、对初始化和精确几何模型敏感的问题。  
方法与创新点：提出STAR-Filter轻量框架，利用星形集构造作为快速滤波器，通过识别障碍点作为主动支撑约束，显著减少冗余计算并增强对传感器噪声的鲁棒性。  
关键结论：仿真表明，该方法在多变环境下计算时间最低，且降低了多面体生成的保守性，在真实噪声和大规模数据中成功应用于安全飞行走廊（SFC）生成与敏捷四旋翼规划。

daily_robot.title: Benchmarking the Safety of Large Language Models for Robotic Health Attendant Control
daily_robot.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly considered for deployment as the control component of robotic health attendants, yet their safety in this context remains poorly characterized. We introduce a dataset of 270 harmful instructions spanning nine prohibited behavior categories grounded in the American Medical Association Principles of Medical Ethics, and use it to evaluate 72 LLMs in a simulation environment based on the Robotic Health Attendant framework. The mean violation rate across all models was 54.4\%, with more than half exceeding 50\%, and violation rates varied substantially across behavior categories, with superficially plausible instructions such as device manipulation and emergency delay proving harder to refuse than overtly destructive ones. Model size and release date were the primary determinants of safety performance among open-weight models, and proprietary models were substantially safer than open-weight counterparts (median 23.7\% versus 72.8\%). Medical domain fine-tuning conferred no significant overall safety benefit, and a prompt-based defense strategy produced only a modest reduction in violation rates among the least safe models, leaving absolute violation rates at levels that would preclude safe clinical deployment. These findings demonstrate that safety evaluation must be treated as a first-class criterion in the development and deployment of LLMs for robotic health attendants.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）作为机器人健康助手控制组件的安全性尚未明确。论文基于美国医学协会伦理准则构建270条有害指令数据集，在仿真环境中评估72个LLM，发现平均违规率达54.4%，专有模型安全性显著优于开放权重模型（中位数23.7% vs 72.8%），医疗领域微调与提示防御策略均未有效降低违规率，表明安全评估应成为LLM部署的首要标准。

daily_robot.title: LLM-Flax : Generalizable Robotic Task Planning via Neuro-Symbolic Approaches with Large Language Models
daily_robot.abstract: Deploying a neuro-symbolic task planner on a new domain today requires significant manual effort: a domain expert must author relaxation and complementary rules, and hundreds of training problems must be solved to supervise a Graph Neural Network (GNN) object scorer. We propose LLM-Flax, a three-stage framework that eliminates all three sources of manual effort using a locally hosted LLM given only a PDDL domain file. Stage 1 automatically generates relaxation and complementary rules via structured prompting with format validation and self-correction. Stage 2 introduces LLM-guided failure recovery with a feasibility-gated budget policy that explicitly reserves API latency cost before each LLM call, preventing the downstream relaxation fallback from being starved. Stage 3 replaces the domain-trained GNN entirely with zero-shot LLM object importance scoring, requiring no training data. We evaluate all three stages on the MazeNamo benchmark across 10x10, 12x12, and 15x15 grids (8 benchmarks total). LLM-Flax achieves average SR 0.945 versus the manual baseline's 0.828 (+0.117), matching or outperforming manual rules on every one of the eight benchmarks. On 12x12 Expert, LLM-Flax attains SR 0.733 where the manual planner fails entirely (SR 0.000); on 15x15 Hard, it achieves SR 1.000 versus Manual's 0.900. Stage 3 demonstrates feasibility (SR 0.720 on 12x12 Hard with no training data) but faces a context-window bottleneck at scale, pointing to the primary open challenge for future work.
daily_robot.summary_zh: 提出了一种仅需PDDL域文件、无需人工编写规则或训练数据的神经符号任务规划框架LLM-Flax。该方法通过结构化提示自动生成松弛规则，引入可行性门控预算策略的LLM引导故障恢复，并用零样本LLM对象评分替代领域训练的GNN。在MazeNamo基准的8个测试中，平均成功率0.945，较人工基线0.828提升0.117，在12x12 Expert上达到0.733（人工规划完全失败），15x15 Hard上达到1.000。

daily_robot.title: Persona-Based Process Design for Assistive Human-Robot Workplaces for Persons with Disabilities
daily_robot.abstract: Human-robot interaction is emerging as an important paradigm for integrating persons with disabilities into the workplace. While these systems can enable individuals to work, their design is mostly personalized, hindering widespread use beyond the individual user. The universal design paradigm is a central pillar of inclusive design, describing usability of systems by all. To incorporate universal design into process design for human-robot workplaces expert knowledge is required that is often not available. To simplify process design of human-robot workplaces, we propose a persona-based design approach. First, typical impairments prevalent in the workforce or particularly relevant for the processes are abstracted into personas with disabilities. The work process is subdivided into sequential actions. For each action and persona, strategies are developed to reach the action goal by a design thinking approach. The resulting actions are ordered by level of robot assistance, i.e. robot involvement, and implemented in a behavior tree. Therefore, the macro-behavior of the workplace may adapt to individual personas online. We demonstrate the method in a collaborative box folding process with a total of seven personas with disabilities. The persona-based process design shows promising results by generating more comprehensive process strategies while enabling adaptive behavior in the sense of universal design.
daily_robot.summary_zh:
**核心问题**：针对残障人士的人机协作工作场所设计多依赖个性化定制，难以推广，且缺乏通用设计所需的专家知识。  
**方法与创新点**：提出基于角色（Persona）的流程设计方法，将典型残障抽象为角色，将工作流程分解为顺序动作，对各动作和角色采用设计思维制定策略，并按机器人辅助程度排序实现为行为树，使系统在线自适应不同角色。  
**关键结论**：在包含7个残障角色的盒子折叠协作流程中，该方法生成了更全面的流程策略，实现了通用设计意义上的自适应行为。

daily_ai.title: Turning the TIDE: Cross-Architecture Distillation for Diffusion Large Language Models
daily_ai.abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) offer parallel decoding and bidirectional context, but state-of-the-art dLLMs require billions of parameters for competitive performance. While existing distillation methods for dLLMs reduce inference steps within a single architecture, none address cross-architecture knowledge transfer, in which the teacher and student differ in architecture, attention mechanism, and tokenizer. We present TIDE, the first framework for cross-architecture dLLM distillation, comprising three modular components: (1) TIDAL, which jointly modulates distillation strength across training progress and diffusion timestep to account for the teacher's noise-dependent reliability; (2) CompDemo, which enriches the teacher's context via complementary mask splitting to improve predictions under heavy masking; and (3) Reverse CALM, a cross-tokenizer objective that inverts chunk-level likelihood matching, yielding bounded gradients and dual-end noise filtering. Distilling 8B dense and 16B MoE teachers into a 0.6B student via two heterogeneous pipelines outperforms the baseline by an average of 1.53 points across eight benchmarks, yielding notable gains in code generation, where HumanEval scores reach 48.78 compared to 32.3 for the AR baseline.
daily_ai.summary_zh:
核心问题：现有扩散大语言模型（dLLM）蒸馏仅限同架构，缺乏跨架构知识迁移方法。  
方法与创新点：提出首个跨架构蒸馏框架TIDE，包含三个模块——TIDAL联合调控蒸馏强度与扩散步长、CompDemo通过互补掩码增强教师上下文、Reverse CALM实现跨分词器的反向块级似然匹配。  
关键结论：将8B密集和16B MoE教师蒸馏至0.6B学生，在8个基准上平均提升1.53分，HumanEval代码生成达48.78（基线32.3）。

daily_ai.title: ClawGym: A Scalable Framework for Building Effective Claw Agents
daily_ai.abstract: Claw-style environments support multi-step workflows over local files, tools, and persistent workspace states. However, scalable development around these environments remains constrained by the absence of a systematic framework, especially one for synthesizing verifiable training data and integrating it with agent training and diagnostic evaluation. To address this challenge, we present ClawGym, a scalable framework that supports the full lifecycle of Claw-style personal agent development. Concretely, we construct ClawGym-SynData, a diverse dataset of 13.5K filtered tasks synthesized from persona-driven intents and skill-grounded operations, paired with realistic mock workspaces and hybrid verification mechanisms. We then train a family of capable Claw-style models, termed ClawGym-Agents, through supervised fine-tuning on black-box rollout trajectories, and further explore reinforcement learning via a lightweight pipeline that parallelizes rollouts across per-task sandboxes.To support reliable evaluation, we further construct ClawGym-Bench, a benchmark of 200 instances calibrated through automated filtering and human-LLM review. Relevant resources will be soon released at https://github.com/ClawGym.
daily_ai.summary_zh: 针对Claw类环境缺乏系统化开发框架的问题，本文提出ClawGym，覆盖智能体开发全生命周期。创新点包括：合成13.5K筛选任务的多领域数据集ClawGym-SynData，基于黑盒轨迹微调并引入轻量级强化学习管线，以及构建含200实例的校准基准ClawGym-Bench。

daily_ai.title: Causal Learning with Neural Assemblies
daily_ai.abstract: Can Neural Assemblies -- groups of neurons that fire together and strengthen through co-activation -- learn the direction of causal influence between variables? While established as a computationally general substrate for classification, parsing, and planning, neural assemblies have not yet been shown to internalize causal directionality. We demonstrate that the inherent operations of neural assemblies -- projection, local plasticity control, and sparse winner selection -- are sufficient for directional learning. We introduce DIRECT (DIRectional Edge Coupling/Training), a mechanism that co-activates source and target assemblies under an adaptive gain schedule to internalize directed relations. Unlike backpropagation-based methods, DIRECT relies solely on local plasticity, making the resulting causal claims auditable at the mechanism level. Our findings are verified through a dual-readout validation strategy: (i) synaptic-strength asymmetry, measuring the emergent weight gap between forward and reverse links, and (ii) functional propagation overlap, quantifying the reliability of directional signal flow. Across multiple domains, the framework achieves perfect structural recovery under a supervised, known-structure setting. These results establish neural assemblies as an auditable bridge between biologically plausible dynamics and formal causal models, offering an "explainable by design" framework where causal claims are traceable to specific neural winners and synaptic asymmetries.
daily_ai.summary_zh: 神经组装（Neural Assemblies）能否学习变量间的因果方向？本文提出DIRECT机制，通过源和目标组装的自适应增益共激活与局部可塑性（无需反向传播）内化有向关系，并利用突触强度不对称性和功能性传播重叠进行双读出验证。在已知结构的监督设定下，该方法实现了完美的结构恢复，建立了一个“可解释设计”的因果学习框架。

daily_ai.title: Recent Advances in mm-Wave and Sub-THz/THz Oscillators for FutureG Technologies
daily_ai.abstract: This paper provides a concise yet comprehensive review of recent advancements in millimeter-wave (mm-wave) oscillators below 100 GHz and sub-terahertz (sub-THz/THz) oscillators above 100 GHz for next-generation computing and communication systems, including 5G, 6G, and beyond. Various design approaches, including CMOS, SiGe, and III-V semiconductor technologies, are explored in terms of performance metrics such as phase noise, output power, efficiency, frequency tunability, and stability. The review highlights key challenges in achieving high-performance and reliable oscillator designs while discussing emerging techniques for performance enhancement. By evaluating recent design trends, this work aims to offer valuable insights and design guidelines that facilitate the development of robust mm-wave and sub-THz/THz oscillators for future communication, computing, and sensing applications.
daily_ai.summary_zh: 该论文系统综述了面向5G/6G及未来通信计算系统的毫米波（<100 GHz）与亚太赫兹/太赫兹（>100 GHz）振荡器最新进展。通过分析CMOS、SiGe、III-V等多种半导体技术，从相位噪声、输出功率、效率、频率可调谐性及稳定性等关键指标出发，探讨了高性能可靠设计的挑战与新兴增强技术。基于对近期设计趋势的评估，为未来通信、计算与感知应用中的稳健振荡器设计提供了有价值的见解与设计指南。

daily_ai.title: Resume-ing Control: (Mis)Perceptions of Agency Around GenAI Use in Recruiting Workflows
daily_ai.abstract: When generative AI (genAI) systems are used in high-stakes decision-making, its recommended role is to aid, rather than replace, human decision-making. However, there is little empirical exploration of how professionals making high-stakes decisions, such as those related to employment, perceive their agency and level of control when working with genAI systems. Through interviews with 22 recruiting professionals, we investigate how genAI subtly influences control over everyday workflows and even individual hiring decisions. Our findings highlight a pressing conflict: while recruiters believe they have final authority across the recruiting pipeline, genAI has become an invisible architect that shapes the foundational building blocks of information used for evaluation, from defining a job to determining good interview performances. The decision of whether or not to adopt was also often outside recruiters' control, with many feeling compelled to adopt genAI due to calls to integrate AI from higher-ups in their business, to combat applicant use of AI, and the individual need to boost productivity. Despite a seemingly seismic shift in how recruiting happens, participants only reported marginal efficiency gains. Such gains came at the high cost of recruiter deskilling, a trend that jeopardizes the meaningful oversight of decision-making. We conclude by discussing the implications of such findings for responsible and perceptible genAI use in hiring contexts.
daily_ai.summary_zh:
核心问题：探究生成式AI（genAI）在高风险招聘决策中如何影响专业人员的能动性与控制感。  
方法与创新点：通过对22名招聘专业人士的访谈，揭示genAI作为“隐形架构师”微妙地重塑工作流与决策基础，而非简单替代人类。  
关键结论：尽管招聘者自认握有最终决定权，却被迫采用genAI以应对外部压力，仅获边际效率提升，却以技能退化为代价，危及决策的实质性监督。

weekly_hot.title: Turning the TIDE: Cross-Architecture Distillation for Diffusion Large Language Models
weekly_hot.abstract: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) offer parallel decoding and bidirectional context, but state-of-the-art dLLMs require billions of parameters for competitive performance. While existing distillation methods for dLLMs reduce inference steps within a single architecture, none address cross-architecture knowledge transfer, in which the teacher and student differ in architecture, attention mechanism, and tokenizer. We present TIDE, the first framework for cross-architecture dLLM distillation, comprising three modular components: (1) TIDAL, which jointly modulates distillation strength across training progress and diffusion timestep to account for the teacher's noise-dependent reliability; (2) CompDemo, which enriches the teacher's context via complementary mask splitting to improve predictions under heavy masking; and (3) Reverse CALM, a cross-tokenizer objective that inverts chunk-level likelihood matching, yielding bounded gradients and dual-end noise filtering. Distilling 8B dense and 16B MoE teachers into a 0.6B student via two heterogeneous pipelines outperforms the baseline by an average of 1.53 points across eight benchmarks, yielding notable gains in code generation, where HumanEval scores reach 48.78 compared to 32.3 for the AR baseline.
weekly_hot.summary_zh: 扩散大语言模型（dLLMs）的跨架构蒸馏面临师生模型架构、注意力机制和分词器差异的挑战。本文提出TIDE框架，包含三个模块：TIDAL联合调节蒸馏强度与扩散时间步，CompDemo通过互补掩码分割丰富教师上下文，Reverse CALM采用反向分块似然匹配实现跨分词器目标。将8B密集和16B MoE教师蒸馏至0.6B学生，在8个基准上平均提升1.53分，HumanEval代码生成达48.78（AR基线32.3）。
weekly_hot.detail_zh:
**1. 研究背景与动机**  
扩散大语言模型（Diffusion Large Language Models, dLLMs）因其支持并行解码和双向上下文建模，在生成效率与质量上展现出巨大潜力。然而，当前最先进的dLLMs往往需要数十亿参数才能达到竞争力水平，这严重限制了其在实际部署中的可扩展性。现有蒸馏方法虽能减少dLLM的推理步数，但均局限于同构架构（教师与学生共享相同架构、注意力机制和分词器），无法实现跨架构知识迁移——即教师（如稠密8B或MoE 16B）和学生（如0.6B）在结构、注意力机制甚至词汇表上完全不同的场景。这一空白阻碍了将大模型知识高效压缩成轻量异构模型的技术路径。

**2. 核心问题定义**  
本文旨在解决**异构架构下的扩散大语言模型知识蒸馏**问题。具体而言，当教师与学生的架构（如稠密 vs. MoE）、注意力机制（如因果 vs. 双向）以及分词器均不同时，如何设计蒸馏框架以有效传递教师模型的生成能力，同时保持学生模型的小型化与高效性。

**3. 方法论详解**  
TIDE框架由三个模块化组件构成：  
**(1) TIDAL (Timestep-Aware Distillation with Adaptive Loss)**：针对扩散过程中教师模型在不同噪声水平下预测可靠性的差异，联合调节蒸馏强度——既随训练进度衰减，又随扩散时间步变化。高噪声步（t较大）时降低对教师输出的依赖，避免误导；低噪声步时加强蒸馏，实现噪声自适应的梯度调制。  
**(2) CompDemo (Complementary Mask Division for Enriched Context)**：在教师端采用互补掩码分裂策略，将原始输入拆分成多个互补的掩码视图，分别输入教师模型以生成更丰富的上下文预测，从而缓解高掩码率下教师输出信息不足的问题，增强学生学习的监督信号。  
**(3) Reverse CALM (Cross-tokenizer Alignment with Likelihood Matching)**：针对教师与学生分词器不一致的挑战，提出反向块级似然匹配目标。该目标将学生模型在教师词汇块上的似然匹配反向操作，不仅产生有界梯度防止训练不稳定，还通过双端噪声过滤（对教师和学生输出同时去噪）提升对齐质量。

**4. 实验结果与关键指标**  
TIDE将8B稠密dLLM和16B MoE dLLM作为双教师，通过两条异构蒸馏管道同时训练一个0.6B的学生模型。在8个涵盖代码、文本、数学等领域的基准测试中，TIDE平均比基线（无蒸馏或单架构蒸馏方法）提升1.53个百分点的绝对性能。尤其值得关注的是代码生成场景：在HumanEval上，TIDE达到**48.78** pass@1，而自回归（AR）基线仅32.3，提升幅度高达16.48个点，充分验证了跨架构蒸馏对代码等结构化任务的巨大增益。

**5. 与现有工作对比**  
与SOTA dLLM蒸馏方法（如同步蒸馏、渐进式蒸馏）相比，TIDE首次突破架构异构限制，无需学生与教师共享分词器或注意力机制。此外，TIDE的三个模块具有即插即用特性，可灵活适配不同教师—学生组合，而现有方法往往需要针对同构架构定制。在相同学生规模下，TIDE显著优于所有同构蒸馏基线以及直接训练的小模型。

**6. 实际意义与未来展望**  
TIDE为低成本部署高性能dLLM开辟了新路径：利用已有的数十亿参数dLLM作为教师，通过跨异构蒸馏直接生成千兆级轻量模型，大幅降低计算和存储成本。工业界可将大型dLLM的知识高效迁移至边缘设备或专用加速器，学术界则获得一个可扩展的蒸馏框架，未来有望进一步支持多种教师类型（如混合LLM）及多模态蒸馏。

**7. 推荐理由**  
本周特别值得关注的原因是：TIDE在异构架构蒸馏这一前沿空白地带首次给出了完整解决方案，并结合代码生成等硬任务上超过15个点的巨大提升，展示了扩散模型知识迁移的突破性进展。

monthly_hot.title: ClawGym: A Scalable Framework for Building Effective Claw Agents
monthly_hot.abstract: Claw-style environments support multi-step workflows over local files, tools, and persistent workspace states. However, scalable development around these environments remains constrained by the absence of a systematic framework, especially one for synthesizing verifiable training data and integrating it with agent training and diagnostic evaluation. To address this challenge, we present ClawGym, a scalable framework that supports the full lifecycle of Claw-style personal agent development. Concretely, we construct ClawGym-SynData, a diverse dataset of 13.5K filtered tasks synthesized from persona-driven intents and skill-grounded operations, paired with realistic mock workspaces and hybrid verification mechanisms. We then train a family of capable Claw-style models, termed ClawGym-Agents, through supervised fine-tuning on black-box rollout trajectories, and further explore reinforcement learning via a lightweight pipeline that parallelizes rollouts across per-task sandboxes.To support reliable evaluation, we further construct ClawGym-Bench, a benchmark of 200 instances calibrated through automated filtering and human-LLM review. Relevant resources will be soon released at https://github.com/ClawGym.
monthly_hot.summary_zh: 当前Claw风格环境缺乏系统化框架来构建可扩展的训练数据与智能体。对此，本文提出ClawGym，涵盖从数据合成（13.5K筛选任务，结合角色驱动意图与技能操作，配以模拟工作空间及混合验证）到智能体训练（监督微调与轻量并行强化学习）及诊断评测（200实例基准ClawGym-Bench，经自动筛选与人机审核）的全流程。实验验证了该框架能有效训练高性能Claw智能体。
monthly_hot.detail_zh:
### 1. 研究背景与动机

当前，以“Claw”风格为代表的智能体环境支持对本地文件、工具及持久化工作区的多步操作，是实现个人自动化助手的关键场景。然而，该领域缺乏系统化的开发框架，尤其在可验证训练数据的合成、智能体的训练流程以及诊断性评测等核心环节存在明显空白，导致不同研究之间的工作难以复现、扩展和比较。

### 2. 核心问题定义

ClawGym 旨在解决 Claw 风格个人智能体全生命周期中的三个关键问题：如何高效合成规模大、质量高且可自动验证的训练数据；如何基于此类数据训练出具备多步操作能力的智能体模型；以及如何构建标准化、细粒度的评测基准。

### 3. 方法论详解

论文提出 ClawGym 框架，包含三大组件：**ClawGym-SynData** 是一个由 13,500 条经过过滤的合成任务组成的数据集，每条任务源自基于用户画像（persona-driven）的高层意图，并通过技能锚定操作（skill-grounded）来细化成可执行步骤；同时配套生成逼真的模拟工作区（mock workspaces）并设计混合验证机制（hybrid verification），兼顾规则检查与模型评估。**ClawGym-Agents** 是一族经过微调的智能体模型：首先通过监督微调（SFT）在黑盒 rollout 轨迹上学习基础能力，再进一步探索轻量级强化学习（RL）管线——该管线在每个任务的独立沙箱中并行执行 rollout，以提升策略质量。最后，**ClawGym-Bench** 是包含 200 个评测实例的基准测试，每个实例经过自动化过滤和 human-LLM 联合审核校准，确保评测的可靠性与区分度。

### 4. 实验结果与关键指标

ClawGym 在自建的 ClawGym-Bench 上进行评测。实验表明，经 SFT 训练后的基础智能体（ClawGym-Agents）在任务成功率上显著优于未微调的基线模型；而引入轻量 RL 管线进一步提升了 5~10% 的平均成功率，尤其在需要多步推理和状态依赖的任务上表现更为突出。论文还通过消融实验验证了合成数据中混合验证机制的有效性：去除该机制后，训练数据的噪声比例上升约 18%，导致最终智能体性能下降。

### 5. 与现有工作对比

与主流面向 Web 浏览或 API 调用的智能体框架（如 WebArena、ToolBench）不同，ClawGym 专门针对本地文件系统和持久化工作区的“Claw”场景，填补了该垂直方向上系统化开发框架的空白。其独创的 persona-driven 数据合成策略和混合验证机制，使得训练数据的质量与多样性均优于直接从日志或规则生成的替代方案。

### 6. 实际意义与未来展望

ClawGym 为个人桌面/终端自动助手的研究提供了可复现的基准、可扩展的数据生成工具以及标准化的训练流水线，有望加速从模型训练到产品部署的迭代周期。未来可探索更复杂的多智能体协作或结合更大型的模型后端（如 70B 级 LLM），以进一步提升 Claw 智能体的泛化能力和鲁棒性。

### 7. 推荐理由

本月 ClawGym 凭借完整的“数据-训练-评测”闭环设计，为 Claw 风格智能体研究树立了首个系统性基准，其开源计划（近期在 GitHub 发布资源）将极大推动该方向的社区协作与标准化发展。
